issues: action buttons occupy ~15% screen width each, together dominating lower-right quadrant and reducing visible game world
large rectangular buttons contrast with 32x32 pixel-art world scale, leaning toward wallpaper feel rather than toy diorama
HUD icon rule ≤24x24 not applicable to full action buttons, but overall HUD footprint is heavy
reasoning: The btn_action_normal_v01 action buttons (召唤/攻击) render as large rounded rectangles (~160×80 px on 1280×640 canvas) in the bottom-right corner. As mobile touch targets this sizing is understandable, but relative to the 32×32 player unit they are disproportionately massive — each button is roughly 5× player width and 2.5× player height. The flat 2D HUD overlay is perspectively consistent (no world-space mismatch), and the style color/border language matches the game aesthetic. However, paired with the top HUD bar and center dialog panel already on screen, the combined UI coverage tips the scene toward 'UI mockup' rather than 'toy diorama with controls'. Score docked for scale imbalance vs. world entities and heavy real-estate footprint, but not failing because category is a UI button (not a world object) and touch usability justifies larger sizing.
